claims.text: Outer execution structure and node-level decision logic are separate architectural layers and may be combined.

evidence_locator.section: Execution structure is not node-level intelligence

claims.text: Direct, Pipeline, Router, State Machine, and DAG answer different execution-control questions and are not a maturity ladder.
Direct is the smallest useful bounded path, while a Pipeline uses a predetermined sequence whose stages should expose explicit contracts and observations.

sections: Direct: use one bounded operation when one is enough
Pipeline: predictable work in a predetermined sequence

claims.text: A production Router needs permission-first controls, abstention or clarification outcomes, controlled overrides, and traceable routing evidence.

sections: Router: choose the path before paying for it
A production router needs an escape path

claims.text: A State Machine makes states, legal transitions, guards, persisted progress, waiting, bounded recovery, and terminal outcomes explicit.

evidence_locator.section: State machine: make progress and legal transitions explicit

claims.text: A DAG models directional task dependencies rather than agent count; concurrency must be bounded and join rules must preserve partial-result policy and provenance.

sections: Join logic is part of the design

claims.text: Event-driven execution and human review are cross-cutting control layers that can wrap Direct, Pipeline, Router, State Machine, or DAG structures.

sections: Event-driven execution: define what starts the work
Human-in-the-loop: insert a governed control point

claims.text: Program generation is an execution technique that still requires a controlling topology plus parsing, policy, sandbox, privilege, limit, and verification controls.

evidence_locator.section: Program generation is an execution technique, not a path topology

claims.text: Architecture selection should start with the smallest sufficient structure and add routing, durable state, dependency graphs, events, and human gates only when their control requirements exist.

sections: How to choose the execution structure
Production controls that belong around every structure
